dynamics: Shaan leads discussion on storytelling craft; Sam asks clarifying questions and shares examples. Focused technical discussion on narrative structure, character development, and audience engagement. Conversational and educational without major conflict.
frameworks: Story structure: hook, setup, inciting incident, rising action, climax, resolution
Character arc: protagonist facing obstacle, internal transformation, external victory
Show don't tell: demonstrate character through action not description
Specificity over generality: concrete details engage more than abstract statements
Tension and release: alternate between emotional buildup and resolution
Relatability: audience needs connection point to protagonist
Stakes escalation: raise emotional or material consequences throughout story
learnings: Storytelling is learnable skill, not innate talent requiring practice and repetition
Best stories feature protagonist struggling against obstacles reflecting audience struggles
Specific sensory details make stories memorable and emotionally resonant
Vulnerability in storytelling creates connection; polished perfection creates distance
Story structure exists across all mediums: podcasts, books, pitches, presentations
Audience cares about transformation and lessons learned, not just events that happened
